name: Nature & Weather
epithet: The remembrances for wind, rain, and the turning sky
title: Nature & Weather Du'as: what to say for wind, thunder, rain and the new moon, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: Authentic du'as for wind, thunder, seeking rain, when it rains, after rain, and the new moon, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.
keywords: dua for rain, dua for wind, dua when it thunders, istisqa dua, dua for the new moon, dua after rain, weather dua, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: The weather was never just weather to the Prophet ﷺ. A wind that picked up, a roll of thunder, the first heavy drops on the roof, the thin curve of a new moon over the rooftops: each one moved him to turn to Allah. Not with worry, but with words. He read the sky as a message and answered it with du'a.

These are those answers. Short lines for the moments the natural world reaches into your day and asks to be noticed. Keep them close, and the ordinary sky becomes a reason to remember the One who holds it up.
intro: There is one for each moment: when the wind rises, when thunder sounds, when you need rain and when too much has come, the moment the first drops fall, the words to say once the rain has passed, and the greeting for the new moon. Reach for whichever the sky hands you.
Most are a single line. Say them in Arabic where you can, lean on the transliteration while you learn, and read the meaning so your heart understands what your tongue is doing.
closing: A storm can make a heart small with fear or large with remembrance, and these words tip it toward the second. The Prophet ﷺ would change colour when the wind blew hard, afraid it carried punishment, and beg for its good while seeking refuge from its harm. That is the posture: never indifferent to the sky, always turning to the One above it.
O Allah, You send the wind and the rain and raise the moon over us. Make every weather a caller to Your remembrance, give us its good, and shelter us from its harm.

faq.q: What do I say when it is raining?
faq.a: The sunnah is to say Allahumma sayyiban nafi'a, asking Allah to make it beneficial rain. Rain is a time when du'a is answered, so it is also a moment to ask Allah for whatever you need.

faq.q: Is there a du'a for thunder?
faq.a: When Abdullah ibn az-Zubayr heard thunder he would stop speaking and glorify Allah, Whom the thunder and the angels praise out of awe of Him. It is reported as his practice, and many keep it when thunder rolls.

faq.q: What should I say when I want it to rain, or to stop?
faq.a: To seek rain there are the du'as of istisqa, asking Allah for beneficial rain that is swift and not delayed. When the rain becomes too heavy, the Prophet ﷺ asked Allah to send it around them and not upon them, onto the hills, valleys and pastures instead.

faq.q: Why greet the new moon with a du'a?
faq.a: A new month is a fresh page. The Prophet ﷺ welcomed each new moon by asking Allah to bring it in with security, faith, peace and safety, and in harmony with what Allah loves and is pleased with.

email.subject: What the Prophet ﷺ said when the sky changed
email.alt_subject: Du'as for wind, thunder, and rain
email.preview: Short remembrances for the wind, thunder, rain, and the new moon, each with its source.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

The weather was never just weather to the Prophet ﷺ. A wind that picked up, a roll of thunder, the first drops on the roof, the thin new moon over the rooftops: each one turned him to Allah, not with worry but with words.

We have gathered those words, the short du'as for the moments the natural world reaches into your day: what to say when the wind rises, when thunder sounds, when you need rain and when too much has come, when the first drops fall, after the rain passes, and when you greet a new moon. Each one comes with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source.

Learn one or two and the ordinary sky becomes a reason to remember the One who holds it up. A storm can make a heart small with fear or large with remembrance; these words tip it toward the second.

The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
The Prophet ﷺ never saw weather as just weather.

Wind, thunder, rain, a new moon: each one turned him to Allah.
social.caption:
Du'as for nature and weather, the words the Prophet ﷺ said when the sky changed.

What to say when the wind rises and when thunder rolls, how to ask for rain and how to ask when there is too much of it, the words for the first drops and for after the rain passes, and the greeting for a new moon.

Most are a single line. Learn one or two and the ordinary sky becomes a reason to remember the One who holds it up.

Save this, and read the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/nature-weather

166.title: When the wind blows
166.note: When the wind picked up, the Prophet ﷺ would ask Allah for its good and seek refuge from its harm, afraid it might carry punishment. A short line for the moment a gust unsettles you.

167.title: When the wind blows (the fuller du'a)
167.note: A longer version: asking for the good of the wind, the good within it, and the good of what it was sent with, and seeking refuge from the matching evils.

168.title: When you hear thunder
168.note: When Abdullah ibn az-Zubayr heard thunder he would stop all conversation and say this, glorifying the One Whom even the thunder and the angels praise out of awe of Him.

169.title: Asking for rain
169.note: A du'a of istisqa: asking Allah for abundant, beneficial rain that comes swiftly and is not delayed.

170.title: O Allah, send us rain
170.note: The Prophet ﷺ said this three times when the people came to him in drought, raising his hands in plea.

171.title: Rain for Your servants and Your land
171.note: Asking Allah to water His servants and their livestock, spread His mercy, and bring the dead earth back to life.

172.title: When the rain begins
172.note: The Prophet ﷺ said this as the rain started falling, asking that it be made beneficial. Rain is a time when du'a is answered, so ask freely.

173.title: After the rain falls
173.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught us to credit the rain to Allah's grace and mercy, not to the stars or the season, returning every blessing to its true Giver.

174.title: When the rain is too heavy
174.note: When the downpour threatened harm, the Prophet ﷺ did not ask for it to stop, but to be turned aside onto the hills, valleys and pastures, away from the people.

175.title: On sighting the new moon
175.note: The Prophet ﷺ greeted each new moon with this, welcoming the new month with security, faith and peace, and in harmony with what Allah loves.
